name: Kubernetes Orchestrator Extension
description: The Kubernetes Orchestrator allows for the remote management of certificate stores defined in a Kubernetes cluster. The following types of Kubernetes resources are supported: kubernetes secrets of `kubernetes.io/tls` or `Opaque` and kubernetes certificates `certificates.k8s.io/v1`
short_description: The Kubernetes Orchestrator allows for remote management of Kubernetes secret types 'Opaque' and 'kubernetes.io/tls' as well as 'certificates.k8s.io/v1' resources.

store_types.Name: K8SCert
store_types.ClientMachineDescription: The Kubernetes cluster name or identifier.

Properties.Name: ServerUsername
Properties.Description: This should be no value or `kubeconfig`

Properties.Name: ServerPassword
Properties.Description: The credentials to use to connect to the K8S cluster API. This needs to be in `kubeconfig` format. Example: https://github.com/Keyfactor/k8s-orchestrator/tree/main/scripts/kubernetes#example-service-account-json

Properties.Name: KubeSecretName
Properties.Description: The name of a specific CSR to inventory. Leave empty or set to '*' to inventory ALL issued CSRs in the cluster.

store_types.Name: K8SCluster
store_types.ClientMachineDescription: This can be anything useful, recommend using the k8s cluster name or identifier.

Properties.Name: IncludeCertChain
Properties.Description: Will default to `true` if not set. If set to `false` only the leaf cert will be deployed. Note: If the certificate in Keyfactor Command does not have a private key, it will be sent in DER format (leaf certificate only), and the chain cannot be included regardless of this setting.

Properties.Name: SeparateChain
Properties.Description: Will default to `false` if not set. Set this to `true` if you want to deploy certificate chain to the `ca.crt` field for Opaque and tls secrets.

Properties.Name: ServerUsername
Properties.Description: This should be no value or `kubeconfig`

Properties.Name: ServerPassword
Properties.Description: The credentials to use to connect to the K8S cluster API. This needs to be in `kubeconfig` format. Example: https://github.com/Keyfactor/k8s-orchestrator/tree/main/scripts/kubernetes#example-service-account-json

store_types.Name: K8SJKS
store_types.ClientMachineDescription: This can be anything useful, recommend using the k8s cluster name or identifier.

Properties.Name: KubeNamespace
Properties.Description: The K8S namespace to use to manage the K8S secret object.

Properties.Name: KubeSecretName
Properties.Description: The name of the K8S secret object.

Properties.Name: KubeSecretType
Properties.Description: DEPRECATED: This property is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. The secret type is now automatically derived from the store type. This defaults to and must be `jks`.

Properties.Name: CertificateDataFieldName
Properties.Description: The field name to use when looking for certificate data in the K8S secret.

Properties.Name: PasswordFieldName
Properties.Description: The field name to use when looking for the JKS keystore password in the K8S secret. This is either the field name to look at on the same secret, or if `PasswordIsK8SSecret` is set to `true`, the field name to look at on the secret specified in `StorePasswordPath`.

Properties.Name: PasswordIsK8SSecret
Properties.Description: Indicates whether the password to the JKS keystore is stored in a separate K8S secret.

Properties.Name: IncludeCertChain
Properties.Description: Will default to `true` if not set. If set to `false` only the leaf cert will be deployed. Note: If the certificate in Keyfactor Command does not have a private key, it will be sent in DER format (leaf certificate only), and the chain cannot be included regardless of this setting.

Properties.Name: StorePasswordPath
Properties.Description: The path to the K8S secret object to use as the password to the JKS keystore. Example: `<namespace>/<secret_name>`

Properties.Name: ServerUsername
Properties.Description: This should be no value or `kubeconfig`

Properties.Name: ServerPassword
Properties.Description: The credentials to use to connect to the K8S cluster API. This needs to be in `kubeconfig` format. Example: https://github.com/Keyfactor/k8s-orchestrator/tree/main/scripts/kubernetes#example-service-account-json

store_types.Name: K8SNS
store_types.ClientMachineDescription: This can be anything useful, recommend using the k8s cluster name or identifier.

Properties.Name: KubeNamespace
Properties.Description: The K8S namespace to use to manage the K8S secret object.

Properties.Name: IncludeCertChain
Properties.Description: Will default to `true` if not set. If set to `false` only the leaf cert will be deployed. Note: If the certificate in Keyfactor Command does not have a private key, it will be sent in DER format (leaf certificate only), and the chain cannot be included regardless of this setting.

Properties.Name: SeparateChain
Properties.Description: Will default to `false` if not set. Set this to `true` if you want to deploy certificate chain to the `ca.crt` field for Opaque and tls secrets.

Properties.Name: ServerUsername
Properties.Description: This should be no value or `kubeconfig`

Properties.Name: ServerPassword
Properties.Description: The credentials to use to connect to the K8S cluster API. This needs to be in `kubeconfig` format. Example: https://github.com/Keyfactor/k8s-orchestrator/tree/main/scripts/kubernetes#example-service-account-json

store_types.Name: K8SPKCS12
store_types.ClientMachineDescription: This can be anything useful, recommend using the k8s cluster name or identifier.

Properties.Name: IncludeCertChain
Properties.Description: Will default to `true` if not set. If set to `false` only the leaf cert will be deployed. Note: If the certificate in Keyfactor Command does not have a private key, it will be sent in DER format (leaf certificate only), and the chain cannot be included regardless of this setting.

Properties.Name: PasswordFieldName
Properties.Description: The field name to use when looking for the PKCS12 keystore password in the K8S secret. This is either the field name to look at on the same secret, or if `PasswordIsK8SSecret` is set to `true`, the field name to look at on the secret specified in `StorePasswordPath`.

Properties.Name: PasswordIsK8SSecret
Properties.Description: Indicates whether the password to the PKCS12 keystore is stored in a separate K8S secret object.

Properties.Name: KubeNamespace
Properties.Description: The K8S namespace to use to manage the K8S secret object.

Properties.Name: KubeSecretName
Properties.Description: The name of the K8S secret object.

Properties.Name: ServerUsername
Properties.Description: This should be no value or `kubeconfig`

Properties.Name: ServerPassword
Properties.Description: The credentials to use to connect to the K8S cluster API. This needs to be in `kubeconfig` format. Example: https://github.com/Keyfactor/k8s-orchestrator/tree/main/scripts/kubernetes#example-service-account-json

Properties.Name: KubeSecretType
Properties.Description: DEPRECATED: This property is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. The secret type is now automatically derived from the store type. This defaults to and must be `pkcs12`.

Properties.Name: StorePasswordPath
Properties.Description: The path to the K8S secret object to use as the password to the PFX/PKCS12 data. Example: `<namespace>/<secret_name>`
